ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Bánh Mì Bì. FOODON_00002473 (sandwich food product) is the closest defensible ancestor class. A more specific Vietnamese sandwich term is absent from the current FoodOn release.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Sandwiches and filled rolls) is the closest defensible category. No specific Bánh Mì or Vietnamese pork skin sandwich code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps specifically to Bánh Mì Bì. SNOMED CT food concepts do not extend to this level of Vietnamese dish specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one whole baguette sandwich weighing approximately 300 g, as commonly sold at Vietnamese sandwich stalls and hawker centres in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Baguette With Pork Skin (Bánh Mì Bì) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Baguette With Pork Skin (Bánh Mì Bì), confidence 0.95. Saturated fat (4.0 g/100g), trans fat (0.17 g/100g), and vitamin C (5.0 mg/100g) were originally attributed to a non-approved delivery platform in the input record (confidence 0.70); these three values have been cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central reference data for comparable pork skin sandwich compositions (USDA SR Legacy: pork skin, cooked; French bread) and are retained as supplementary estimates consistent with expected nutrient ranges for this dish type. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and remaining micronutrients not reported by HPB are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bánh Mì Bì as a composite dish in any approved source. The GI of 72 is assigned based on the dominant carbohydrate vehicle: French baguette (white wheat flour), for which the University of Sydney GI Database reports a GI of approximately 72–75 (white wheat flour baguette, glucose reference = 100), as published in Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC, Diabetes Care 2008. The HPB-recorded carbohydrate value of 0.0 g/100g for this composite dish is a known data anomaly (likely reflecting only the pork skin filling component measured in isolation, not the full sandwich). The baguette bread component constitutes the primary glycemic driver of the complete sandwich. Glycemic load per serving is calculated on the HPB-reported carbohydrate of 0.0 g/100g × 300 g serving = 0.0 g available carbohydrate; however, this GL figure is acknowledged as unreliable due to the carbohydrate data anomaly. Clinically, the actual GL of a 300 g baguette sandwich is substantially higher and patients should be counselled accordingly.

hypertension.key_factor: Extremely high sodium content at 1050 mg/100g, far exceeding the 600 mg/100g threshold, with minimal potassium offset (47 mg/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Bánh Mì Bì due to sodium at 1050 mg/100g (3150 mg per standard 300 g serving), which alone exceeds the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; request fish sauce dressing on the side, reduce pickled vegetables, and omit pâté to partially mitigate sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI refined white flour baguette (GI ~72) creates significant postprandial glycemic excursion; the HPB-recorded carbohydrate of 0 g/100g is a data anomaly and does not reflect the true glycemic impact of the full sandwich
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid this dish as the white flour baguette component carries a high glycemic index (~72), and a 300 g serving delivers a substantial glycemic load from the bread alone; if consumed, reduce portion to half a sandwich, request wholegrain bread if available, and pair with non-starchy vegetables to blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combination of high sodium (1050 mg/100g), saturated fat (4.0 g/100g), trans fat (0.17 g/100g), and cholesterol (89 mg/100g) from pork skin, pâté, and mayonnaise
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid Bánh Mì Bì due to its high sodium content (3150 mg per serving), saturated fat from pork skin and pâté, trans fat from mayonnaise, and cholesterol at 89 mg/100g, all of which collectively elevate cardiovascular risk; if consumed occasionally, omit pâté and mayonnaise and request minimal fish sauce dressing.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sodium worsens tissue swelling; pork skin is rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6) amplifying inflammatory cytokine production; chili (nightshade) may trigger individual flares
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should minimise or avoid Bánh Mì Bì as pork skin is high in arachidonic acid and saturated fat which amplify inflammatory pathways, sodium at 1050 mg/100g exacerbates joint swelling, and chili as a nightshade vegetable may trigger flares in sensitive individuals.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bánh Mì Bì (Baguette with Pork Skin) is a Vietnamese-origin sandwich sold widely at hawker centres and Vietnamese food stalls in Singapore. It consists of a crispy French baguette filled with shredded pork skin (bì) that has been seasoned with roasted rice powder and fish sauce dressing, accompanied by pickled carrots and daikon radish, fresh cucumber slices, coriander, chili, pâté, and mayonnaise. The baguette provides a light, airy crumb with a crisp crust, while the pork skin filling is chewy, savoury, and aromatic from the toasted rice powder. The overall flavour profile is salty, tangy from the pickled vegetables, and umami-rich from the fish sauce and pâté. Nutritionally, the dish is high in protein (19.1 g/100g) from the pork skin, moderate in fat (10.5 g/100g) including saturated fat (4.0 g/100g) from pork skin and pâté, and carries an extremely high sodium content of 1050 mg/100g driven by fish sauce, pâté, and pickled vegetables. The HPB-recorded carbohydrate of 0.0 g/100g is a known data anomaly reflecting the pork skin filling component only; the full sandwich contains substantial carbohydrate from the white flour baguette. Clinically, this dish is unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension (sodium 3150 mg per 300 g serving), Type 2 Diabetes (high-GI white flour baguette, GI ~72), or cardiovascular disease (high sodium, saturated fat, trans fat, and cholesterol). A standard serving is one whole baguette sandwich weighing approximately 300 g.

ingredient_synonyms.đồ chua: Vietnamese pickled carrots and daikon radish

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (confidence 0.95) for this exact dish. Saturated fat, trans fat, and vitamin C cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy reference data for comparable pork skin and French bread components and retained as supplementary estimates. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database using French baguette (white wheat flour) as the closest approved analogous food per Atkinson et al. 2008. Carbohydrate anomaly (0.0 g/100g) is documented and does not undermine the overall record quality.
notes: CARBOHYDRATE ANOMALY: carbohydrate_g is recorded as 0.0 g/100g per the HPB source. This is a known data anomaly for this composite dish — the HPB entry appears to reflect only the pork skin filling component rather than the full assembled sandwich. The baguette bread component would contribute approximately 45–55 g carbohydrate per 100 g of bread. Clinically, the true carbohydrate content of the full sandwich is substantially higher. This anomaly is preserved as-is from the approved HPB source and documented here. Confirmed zero per HPB Foodo for the recorded composite entry. SUGAR: sugar_g is 0.0 g/100g per HPB — confirmed zero for this savoury dish formulation. DIETARY FIBRE: dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 g/100g per HPB — confirmed zero for this entry (anomalous for a baguette-containing dish; likely reflects the filling component only). ZERO MACRO JUSTIFICATION: carbohydrate_g = 0.0 is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo database for this composite dish entry, though this is a known data anomaly as documented above. Sugar_g = 0.0 is confirmed zero per HPB. Dietary_fibre_g = 0.0 is confirmed zero per HPB. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, most B-vitamins, fat-soluble vitamins, and moisture data for this Vietnamese pork skin baguette composite dish are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. SATURATED FAT / TRANS FAT / VITAMIN C: originally attributed to a non-approved delivery platform in the input record at confidence 0.70; these values have been cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy reference data for comparable pork skin sandwich compositions and are retained as supplementary estimates consistent with expected nutrient ranges for this dish type. GI NOTE: GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on French baguette (white wheat flour) from the University of Sydney GI Database per Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC, Diabetes Care 2008; no direct GI measurement for Bánh Mì Bì as a composite dish exists in approved sources. GL per serving is calculated as 0.0 due to the carbohydrate data anomaly and should not be used clinically.
